name: psyllium (plantago ovata)

mechanism: Proposed mechanism (well-aligned with bulk laxative pharmacology): Bulk-forming agents swell in liquid, increasing stool bulk and promoting softer stool; EMA indicates effect starts 12–24 hours later and emphasizes adequate fluid intake. Delayed absorption of co-administered medicines is consistent with mucilage/bulk effects.
activeCompounds: arabinoxylan is a characterized polysaccharide class used to describe major psyllium/Plantago mucilage components
PubChem contains indexed entries relevant to psyllium husk/Plantago seed materials and arabinoxylan references.
safetyNotes: EMA contraindications include GI structural/functional disorders (e.g., existing/potential intestinal blockage/ileus, abnormal constrictions, paralysis of intestine, megacolon), swallowing difficulties, and undiagnosed rectal bleeding; special warnings: adequate fluid is required; inadequate fluid can cause obstruction/choking; discontinue and seek advice if abdominal pain or irregularity occurs. Use not recommended under age 6.

sources.title: Community herbal monograph on *Plantago ovata* Forssk., semen (EMA)
*Plantago ovata* Forssk. - Plants of the World Online (Kew)

description: Medicinal material is dried ripe *Plantago ovata* seeds (ispaghula seed); monograph also recognizes powdered herbal substance as a preparation form.
dosage: EMA posology: - Adolescents/adults/elderly: 8–40 g herbal substance/preparation daily in 2–3 single doses. - Children 6–12 years: 4–25 g daily in 2–3 single doses. Duration guidance: if constipation does not resolve within 3 days, consult clinician.
interactions: EMA: take at least ½ to 1 hour before or after other medicines, due to potential delay in absorption.
preparation: Mix with sufficient liquid (examples include water, milk, fruit juice) and swallow; maintain adequate fluid intake; avoid taking immediately before bedtime; avoid inhaling powder during preparation to reduce sensitization risk.
region: POWO lists native range as SE Spain, North Africa to India and Somalia, and SW/S Central U.S.A. to N. Mexico.
sideEffects: potential choking if not taken with water
summary: EMA monograph supports well-established use of *Plantago ovata* seed as a bulk-forming laxative for habitual constipation and for situations where easy defecation with soft stool is desirable. Key safety/quality point: must be taken with sufficient liquid; inadequate fluid can cause choking or GI obstruction. Also, it can delay absorption of other medicines, so timing separation is advised.
